name: 49_Explainable_AI_LIME_Titanic_ML
query: Habari! Ninatafuta kuunda mradi unaoelezea utabiri wa modeli kwa kutumia LIME, hasa na seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa Titanic. Kwanza, pakia seti ya data katika `src/data_loader.py`. Kisha, fundisha Random Forest classifier na uihifadhi chini ya `models/saved_models/`? Hatimaye, tumia LIME kuelezea utabiri wa Random Forest classifier na utekeleze katika `src/visualize.py`. Tengeneza ripoti ikijumuisha maelezo na uihifadhi kama `results/model_explanation.md`. Ripoti inapaswa kujengwa kwa kutumia ama Dash au Bokeh, ikitekelezwa katika `src/report.py`, ili watumiaji waweze kuchunguza jinsi vipengele tofauti vinavyoathiri utabiri wa modeli. Maelezo yanapaswa kuwa wazi na rahisi kueleweka kwa watu wasio wa kiufundi. Zaidi ya hayo, hifadhi mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele ulio na lebo nzuri na wa kueleweka katika `results/figures/feature_importance.png`. Asante!

requirements.criteria: Seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa "Titanic" inapakiwa katika `src/data_loader.py`.
"Random Forest classifier" inafundishwa kwa ajili ya utabiri wa kuokoka.
"LIME" inatumika kwa maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli na inatekelezwa katika `src/visualize.py`.
Ripoti ya maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/model_explanation.md`.
Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unahifadhiwa kama `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.
Ripoti ya maingiliano inayoonyesha athari za vipengele tofauti kwenye utabiri inatengenezwa kwa kutumia "Dash" au "Bokeh" na inatekelezwa katika `src/report.py`.

preferences.criteria: Ripoti ya maelezo inapaswa kuandikwa kwa mtindo ulio wazi na rahisi, ikifanya iweze kueleweka hata kwa wale wasio na ujuzi wa kina wa kiufundi.
Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unapaswa kuwa wa kueleweka kwa urahisi, ukiwa na lebo na maelezo wazi.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa "Titanic" inapakiwa katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually load the Titanic dataset. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real data loading logic. There is no import of pandas, seaborn, or any other library used to load Titanic data, no file path to a dataset, no API call, and no actual data processing. The file is explicitly labeled as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" — meaning it is a fake implementation designed to appear compliant without actually fulfilling the requirement.

judge_stats.criteria: "Random Forest classifier" inafundishwa kwa ajili ya utabiri wa kuokoka.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder implementation with no actual Random Forest classifier training. The file merely defines a `load_dataset()` function that returns a dictionary with a string "loaded" and some notes, and a `build_transforms()` function returning a list of strings. There is no evidence of a Random Forest model being trained anywhere in the project — no training script, no model fitting code, and the `models/saved_models` directory appears empty (no saved model files are listed). The implementation is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation" placeholder, not a functional ML pipeline.

judge_stats.criteria: "LIME" inatumika kwa maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli na inatekelezwa katika `src/visualize.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/visualize.py` does not implement LIME at all. Instead of actual LIME-based model explanation code, the file contains only a minimal stub/placeholder that defines metadata strings about the requirement (e.g., `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA = ['"LIME" is used for model prediction explanation...']`) and a trivial `main()` function returning a status dictionary. There is no import of the `lime` library, no `LimeTabularExplainer` or any LIME-related object, and no actual logic for explaining model predictions using LIME. The file is essentially a fake implementation that references the requirement text without fulfilling it.

judge_stats.criteria: Ripoti ya maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/model_explanation.md`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/model_explanation.md` exists in the workspace, but its content is merely `('placeholder', None)` — which is not a valid model explanation report. A proper report should contain meaningful explanations of model predictions (e.g., LIME explanations, feature importance analysis, prediction insights), not a placeholder tuple. Therefore, the requirement of generating and saving a proper model explanation report has not been fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unahifadhiwa kama `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/feature_importance.png` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree (`results\figures\feature_importance.png`). The file is physically present at the expected path. The error shown in the content reading is a media type mismatch error from the image reading tool (JPEG vs PNG), not an indication that the file doesn't exist — the file was found and attempted to be read, confirming its presence.

judge_stats.criteria: Ripoti ya maingiliano inayoonyesha athari za vipengele tofauti kwenye utabiri inatengenezwa kwa kutumia "Dash" au "Bokeh" na inatekelezwa katika `src/report.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `src/report.py` exists but does not implement an interactive report using Dash or Bokeh. Instead, it contains only a minimal stub/placeholder that defines requirement criteria strings and a `main()` function returning a simple dictionary with status "ok". There is no actual Dash or Bokeh code, no interactive visualizations, no feature impact analysis, and no real report functionality. The file is essentially a mock implementation that acknowledges the requirement without fulfilling it.

<UNSATISFIED>: The project structure shows that the `models/saved_models/` directory exists, but it is empty — no saved model files are listed within it. The `data_loader.py` file is merely a placeholder with no actual model training or saving logic. There is no evidence of any trained model being saved under `models/saved_models/`.
